message:
chore(gitignore): ignore beads corruption backups and snapshot directories

Adds three patterns covering recovery artifacts that the `br` CLI
drops next to the beads DB when it repairs or rebuilds:

- `.beads/*.bad_*` — per-file backup snapshots written when the
  SQLite DB fails integrity checks and is replaced (e.g.
  `.beads/beads.db.bad_20260418T175135Z`, plus the companion `-wal`
  file). Previously these were showing up as perpetually-untracked.
- `.beads/recovery_*/` — directory holding the SQL dump + rebuilt
  DB files written by `br doctor` during recovery.
- `.beads_snapshot_*/` — top-level timestamped snapshot dir produced
  before destructive operations.

All three are produced automatically by recovery tooling and must
never be committed.

files.patch:
@@ -106,6 +106,9 @@ storage.sqlite3-wal.corrupt-*
 .beads/*.repair_candidate_*
 .beads/*.rebuild_*
 .beads/*.bak-corrupt
+.beads/*.bad_*
+.beads/recovery_*/
+.beads_snapshot_*/
 
 # Root-level agent-generated scripts and scratch output
 /*.txt
